channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: Finland’s prime minister says authorities are investigating an interruption in a power cable under the Baltic Sea between his country and Estonia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 46
item.description: A Taliban government official said the airstrikes Pakistan launched on Afghanistan Tuesday killed 46 people, including women and children, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner crashes in Kazakhstan, killing 38 with 29 survivors, officials say
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, killing 38 people and leaving 29 survivors, a Kazakh official said.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Desi Bouterse, a dictator convicted of murder who twice ruled Suriname, has died at 79
item.description: PARAMARIBO, Suriname (AP) — Desi Bouterse, a military strongman who led a 1980 coup in the former Dutch colony of Suriname then returned to power by election three decades later despite charges of drug smuggling and murder, has died. He was 79.
item.dc:creator: GEROLD ROZENBLAD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A baby froze to death overnight in Gaza as Israel and Hamas trade accusations of ceasefire delays
item.description: A baby girl froze to death overnight in Gaza, while Israel and Hamas accused each other of complicating ceasefire efforts that could wind down the 14-month war.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Tia Goldenberg And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelensky condemns Putin for 'inhumane' Christmas Day attack
item.description: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelensky condemned Russia’s President Vladimir Putin for greenlighting an “inhumane” Christmas Day attack, including missiles and drones, that damaged Ukraine’s energy infrastructure and left thousands with power outages.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Pope calls for end to global wars, divisions in Christmas message
item.description: Pope Francis called for an end to global wars and divisiveness in his annual Christmas Day message, given Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, The Hill

item.title: US citizen imprisoned in Russia given new 15-year sentence in wake of espionage conviction
item.description: A Russian-born U.S. citizen who was already behind bars in Russia on a bribery conviction has been handed a new 15-year sentence for espionage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey's soaring costs are creating a 'lost generation' of kids forced to help their families get by
item.description: One-third of Turkey's children live in poverty, with many forced to work to support their struggling families.
item.dc:creator: Robert Badendieck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A Haiti gang attack on the reopening of a main hospital kills 2 reporters and a police officer
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Two reporters were killed and several were wounded Tuesday in a gang attack in Haiti on the reopening of Port-au-Prince’s biggest public hospital, the country's online media association said. A police officer was also killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian ship sinks in Mediterranean after explosion
item.description: A Russian cargo ship sank Monday in the Mediterranean Sea leaving two of the 16 crew members unaccounted for, according to the country’s authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: After 20 years, the post-tsunami generation stays vigilant for future disasters
item.description: LHOKNGA, Indonesia (AP) — Qurrata Ayuni, a 28-year-old survivor of the earthquake and tsunami that devastated her hometown two decades ago, has transformed her resilience into purpose.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, ACHMAD IBRAHIM, and FADLAN SYAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Laredo getting $20M in federal funds to help ailing water infrastructure system
item.description: The border city of Laredo, Texas, is getting an infusion of $20 million to help its troubled water infrastructure system, U.S. Rep. Henry Cuellar announced.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Denmark to boost Greenland defense amid Trump comments
item.description: Denmark announced a major new package to boost the security of Greenland in a move the Danish defense minister called "ironic" because it came just two days after President-elect Trump called for U.S. ownership of the Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russian court sentences US citizen to 15 years in jail for espionage
item.description: A Russian court has sentenced a U.S. citizen to 15 years in jail for espionage on Tuesday, according to Russian state media outlets. Eugene Spector, who was born in Russia but later moved to the U.S. and got his U.S. citizenship, was already serving a 3 1/2-year sentence for bribery.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: France has a new government, again. Politics and crushing debt complicate next steps
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s president and prime minister managed to form a new government just in time for the holidays. Now comes the hard part.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN investigative team says Syria's new authorities 'very receptive' to probe of Assad war crimes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. organization assisting in investigating the most serious crimes in Syria said Monday the country’s new authorities were “very receptive” to its request for cooperation during a just-concluded visit to Damascus, and it is preparing to deploy.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mulvaney on Trump, Panama Canal: It’s a negotiation
item.description: Former White House chief of staff Mick Mulvaney said Monday that President-elect Trump's recent provocations about the U.S. possibly retaking control of the Panama Canal are part of a negotiating tactic to get the rates down.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: France has a new government. Now it must fix the budget while avoiding collapse
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron’s office announced a new government Monday, after the previous Cabinet collapsed in a historic vote prompted by fighting over the country's budget.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Magdeburg mourns Christmas market attack victims as fears swirl of deeper German social divisions
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Mourners laid flowers near the scene of the deadly Christmas market attack on Monday as investigators puzzled over the motive of the suspect and his previous encounters with authorities were scrutinized, while fears swirled that the rampage could deepen divisions in German
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Christmas in Puerto Rico is a 45-day celebration with caroling, festive decorations, family feasts and more
item.description: Christmas in Puerto Rico is spent with loved ones singing carols, sitting around the table for a meal and exchanging gifts. There celebrations last from Thanksgiving to mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in what appeared to be "friendly fire", but were found alive, the U.S. military said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation
